entries.definition:
كتاب القاف

قتع - قتل

(قتل) القاف والتاء واللام أصلٌ صحيح يدلُّ على إذلالٍ وإماتةٍ. يقال: قتَلَهُ قَتْلاً. والقِتْلَة: الحالُ يُقْتَلُ عليها. يقال قَتَله قِتلةَ سَوء. والقَتْلة: المرّة الواحدة. ومَقاتِلُ الإنسان: المواضع التي إذا أُصِيبت قَتَله ذلك. ومن ذلك: قتلتُ الشيءَ خُبراً وعِلْماً. قال الله سبحانه: ] وَمَا قَتَلُوهُ يَقِيناً [ [النساء 157]. [ويقال: تقتَّلت الجاريةُ للرّجُل حتَّى عَشِقَها، كأنَّها خَضَعَتْ له. قال ]:

تنسَّكْتِ، ما هذا بفعل النواسِكِ

تقَتَّلتِ لي حتَّى إذا ما قتلتِني

(25:67) وَالَّذِينَ إِذَا أَنْفَقُوا لَمْ يُسْرِفُوا وَلَمْ يَقْتُرُوا and those who, when they
spend, they neither squander nor become stingy.

قَتَر qatar [n.] darkness, dejection, gloom, melancholy, distress
(10:26) لِلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا الْحُسْنَى وَزِيَادَةٌ وَلَا يَرْهَقُ وُجُوهَهُمْ قَتَرٌ وَلَا ذِلَّةٌ for those
who do good is goodness [itself] and more besides is [a reward
most fair], and neither darkness nor abasement shall besmirch
their reputation [lit. cover their faces].

قَتَرَة qataratun [n.] distress, dejection, gloom, melancholy,
darkness (80:41) تَرْهَقُهَا قَتَرَةٌ smothered in darkness.

قَتُور qatūr [quasi-intens. act. part.] very stingy, given to
meanness, miserly, very grudging (17:100) لَوْ أَنْتُمْ تَمْلِكُونَ خَزَائِنَ رَحْمَةِ
رَبِّي إِذًا لَأَمْسَكْتُمْ خَشْيَةَ الْإِنْفَاقِ وَكَانَ الْإِنْسَانُ قَتُورًا if you possessed the coffers
of mercy of my Lord, you would hold back for fear of spending—
man is ever stingy.

مُقْتِر muqtir [quasi-act. part.] one who is needy, one in strained
circumstances (2:236) وَعَلَى الْمُوسِعِ قَدَرُهُ وَعَلَى الْمُقْتِرِ قَدَرُهُ and
make provision for them [at the termination of marriage], the
affluent according to his means and the strained one according to
his.
entries.definition_english:
(25:67) وَالَّذِينَ إِذَا أَنْفَقُوا لَمْ يُسْرِفُوا وَلَمْ يَقْتُرُوا and those who, when they

مُقْتِر muqtir [quasi-act. part.] one who is needy, one in strained

make provision for them [at the termination of marriage], the
affluent according to his means and the strained one according to
his.

entries.definition:
q–t–l to kill, killing, slaughter; to fight; to put into hardship; to
curse; to inquire, to look deeply; to quench a thirst; to be
experienced; (of an animal) to be trained; to be worldly wise; to
work very hard. Of this root, 11 forms occur in 170 places in the
Qur’an: قَتَلَ qatala 63 times; قُتِلَ qutila 20 times; قَتَّلَ qattala
twice; قُتِّلَ quttila twice; قَاتَلَ qātala 51 times; قُوتِلَ qūtila three
times; اقْتَتَلَ ʾiqtatala four times; قَتْل qatl 10 times; تَقْتِيل taqtīl once;
قِتَال qitāl 13 times and قَتْلَى qatlā once.

قَتَلَ qatala u [v. trans.] 1 to kill (5:32) مَنْ قَتَلَ نَفْسًا بِغَيْرِ نَفْسٍ أَوْ
فَسَادٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ فَكَأَنَّمَا قَتَلَ النَّاسَ جَمِيعًا whoever kills a person—not in
retribution for [the killing of] another nor for spreading
corruption in the land—it is as if he has killed all humankind;
*(4:29) وَلَا تَقْتُلُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ do not kill each other 2 to suppress one’s
own lusts (in one interpretation of 2:54) إِنَّكُمْ ظَلَمْتُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ بِاتِّخَاذِكُمُ الْعِجْلَ
فَتُوبُوا إِلَى بَارِئِكُمْ فَاقْتُلُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ you have wronged yourselves by taking

737
entries.definition_english:
q–t–l to kill, killing, slaughter; to fight; to put into hardship; to
curse; to inquire, to look deeply; to quench a thirst; to be
experienced; (of an animal) to be trained; to be worldly wise; to
work very hard. Of this root, 11 forms occur in 170 places in the

times; اقْتَتَلَ ʾiqtatala four times; قَتْل qatl 10 times; تَقْتِيل taqtīl once;
قِتَال qitāl 13 times and قَتْلَى qatlā once.

قَتَلَ qatala u [v. trans.] 1 to kill (5:32) مَنْ قَتَلَ نَفْسًا بِغَيْرِ نَفْسٍ أَوْ
فَسَادٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ فَكَأَنَّمَا قَتَلَ النَّاسَ جَمِيعًا whoever kills a person—not in
retribution for [the killing of] another nor for spreading
corruption in the land—it is as if he has killed all humankind;
*(4:29) وَلَا تَقْتُلُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ do not kill each other 2 to suppress one’s
own lusts (in one interpretation of 2:54) إِنَّكُمْ ظَلَمْتُمْ أَنْفُسَكُمْ بِاتِّخَاذِكُمُ الْعِجْلَ
فَتُوبُوا إِلَى بَارِئِكُمْ فَاقْتُلُوا أَنْفُسَكُمْ you have wronged yourselves by taking

737

entries.definition:
the calf [for worship], so turn to your Creator and cleanse your
souls (or, kill [the guilty amongst] yourselves).

قُتِلَ qutila [pass. v.] 1 to be killed (2:154) وَلَا تَقُولُوا لِمَنْ يُقْتَلُ فِي سَبِيلِ
اللَّهِ أَمْوَاتٌ بَلْ أَحْيَاءٌ وَلَكِنْ لَا تَشْعُرُونَ do not describe those who are killed
for God’s cause as dead; they are alive, though you do not realise
it 2 to be defeated (in one interpretation of 3:154) يَقُولُونَ لَوْ كَانَ لَنَا مِنَ
الْأَمْرِ شَيْءٌ مَا قُتِلْنَا هَاهُنَا they say, ‘If we had a say in the matter, we
would not have been defeated (or, killed) here’ 3 [interjection]
woe to, perish!, confound (him)! (80:17) قُتِلَ الْإِنْسَانُ مَا أَكْفَرَهُ perish
man, how ungrateful he is!; (74:18–19) إِنَّهُ فَكَّرَ وَقَدَّرَ فَقُتِلَ كَيْفَ قَدَّرَ he
planned and plotted; confound him, how he plotted!

قَتَّلَ qattala [v. II, trans.; pass. v. قُتِّلَ quttil] to slay all, to kill in
large numbers, to exterminate (7:127) سَنُقَتِّلُ أَبْنَاءَهُمْ وَنَسْتَحْيِي نِسَاءَهُمْ we
will exterminate their male children and spare their women.

قَاتَلَ qātala [v. III] I [trans.] to fight (5:24) فَاذْهَبْ أَنْتَ وَرَبُّكَ فَقَاتِلَا إِنَّا
هَاهُنَا قَاعِدُونَ so you and your Lord go in and fight; we are sitting
here II [trans.] to combat, to fight against, to attack (2:191) فَإِنْ
قَاتَلُوكُمْ فَاقْتُلُوهُمْ if they do fight you, kill them; *(9:30) قَاتَلَهُمُ اللَّهُ may
God confound them!

قُوتِلَ qūtila [v. III, pass.] to be fought, to be attacked (22:39) أُذِنَ
لِلَّذِينَ يُقَاتَلُونَ بِأَنَّهُمْ ظُلِمُوا permitted [to defend themselves] are those
who are being attacked, because they have been wronged.

اقْتَتَلَ ʾiqtatala [v. VIII, intrans.] to fight against one another, to
battle against one another (49:9) وَإِنْ طَائِفَتَانِ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ اقْتَتَلُوا فَأَصْلِحُوا
بَيْنَهُمَا and if two parties of the believers fall to fighting, put things
right between them.

قَتْل qatl [v. n.; n.] killing, slaughter (33:16) لَنْ يَنْفَعَكُمُ الْفِرَارُ إِنْ فَرَرْتُمْ
مِنَ الْمَوْتِ أَوِ الْقَتْلِ running away will not benefit you if you flee from
death or killing [being killed].

تَقْتِيل taqtīl [v. n.] intense killing, slaughtering (also
imprecationally) (33:61) مَلْعُونِينَ أَيْنَمَا ثُقِفُوا أُخِذُوا وَقُتِّلُوا تَقْتِيلًا accursed,
wherever they are found, they will (or, may they) be seized and
slain absolutely.

قِتَال qitāl [v. n.; n.] fighting, combat (33:25) وَكَفَى اللَّهُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ الْقِتَالَ

738
entries.definition_english:
the calf [for worship], so turn to your Creator and cleanse your
souls (or, kill [the guilty amongst] yourselves).
